methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Sabanto is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables spanning private robotics firms including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, alongside 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage and sector. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying a valuation of $40M. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company robotics cohort yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The peer set exhibits substantial dispersion; the high end is anchored by 1X Technologies at $10.0B (Series B), while the lower quartile includes Collaborative Robotics and Fairmat at $500M and $350M respectively.

The triangulated range across both methods supports a valuation between $34M and $1.2B, with a median of $558M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty in comparability: peer valuations vary by stage advancement, technology differentiation, and fundraising momentum. Material sensitivities include Sabanto's technical progress relative to Series B peers, revenue or deployment traction data not provided here, and the sustainability of current robotics sector multiples. Additional clarity on unit economics and customer concentration would narrow the band.
